tool.name: compseq
tool.summary: Report deterministic residue composition counts and frequencies for sequence records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `compseq` reports residue composition for each input record plus one aggregate summary across all records. The EMBOSS-RS v1 surface intentionally keeps the report deterministic and simple: count normalized non-gap symbols, preserve ambiguity or stop symbols as observed, and compute frequencies over the non-gap denominator only.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local sequence input path. Inputs are loaded through the shared EMBOSS-RS readers for FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank, and may contain nucleotide, protein, or mixed record sets.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with columns `scope`, `record`, `molecule`, `length`, `residue`, `count`, and `frequency`. `scope` is either `record` or `aggregate`. Aggregate rows use `record=ALL` and `molecule=mixed` in the first release.

sections.title: Statistics Model
sections.content: Residues are normalized case-insensitively before counting. Gap symbols `-` are ignored and excluded from the frequency denominator. All other normalized symbols, including ambiguity codes such as `N` and stop symbols such as `*`, are counted exactly as observed. Frequencies are reported as fractions over all non-gap symbols in the corresponding record or aggregate set.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `compseq` composition example in view and compares the EMBOSS-RS tabular payload against a committed expected output. The comparison validates the governed symbol-counting and non-gap frequency rules for the committed nucleotide fixture.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs compseq`. Validation currently covers nucleotide composition, protein composition, ambiguity and stop-symbol handling, per-record plus aggregate reporting, and empty-input failure.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not infer richer chemistry-aware residue classes or reject mixed record sets. Composition is purely symbol-based after shared sequence normalization.

artifacts.label: Nucleotide composition fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed nucleotide fixture used for deterministic composition validation.

examples.title: Compute per-record and aggregate composition
examples.description: Reports normalized residue counts and frequencies for each input record plus one aggregate summary across the whole input set.

expected_outputs.label: Residue composition table
expected_outputs.description: A stable tabular report containing per-record and aggregate residue counts and non-gap frequencies.
